description_long: This object is a tall, slender obelisk-style grave marker, commonly found in graveyards and cemeteries. It features a rectangular base with a slightly raised platform, supporting a tapering, four-sided shaft that culminates in a pointed top. The surface appears to be made of weathered stone, with a mottled gray coloration and subtle textural variations, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The structure is solid and monolithic, with no visible inscriptions, engravings, or decorative elements. Its geometric form is simple and austere, evoking a sense of solemnity and permanence. The overall design is utilitarian, serving as a dignified memorial within an urban or park-like cemetery setting.
description: A tall, gray stone obelisk grave marker with a rectangular base and a tapering shaft, featuring a weathered, austere appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the narrow face of the obelisk and the stepped rectangular base.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the wider face of the obelisk and the full width of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, showing the narrow face and the stepped base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, displaying the wide face and base.
